texto: Murderers don't even know that murder happens.
explicacao: Citação de David Rabe sobre limites e escolhas conscientes. “Murderers don't even know that murder happens.” articula reflexao, murder, murderers em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como reflexao.

texto: There’s little back roads and little towns sometimes I never heard of them. I start to expect the gas station attendants to know me when I arrive. I get excited that I’ve been there before. I want them to welcome me. I’m disappointed when they don’t. Something that I don’t want to be true starts lookin’ like it’s al that’s true only I don’t know what it is. No. No. I need my marriage. I come here to tell you. I got to stay married. I’m lost without her.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a David Rabe: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “There’s little back roads and little towns sometimes I never heard of them. I start to expect the…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que tempo, hurlyburly, loneliness pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de casamento e termino.

frase_original: There’s little back roads and little towns sometimes I never heard of them. I start to expect the gas station attendants to know me when I arrive. I get excited that I’ve been there before. I want them to welcome me. I’m disappointed when they don’t. Something that I don’t want to be true starts lookin’ like it’s al that’s true only I don’t know what it is. No. No. I need my marriage. I come here to tell you. I got to stay married. I’m lost without her.
